gaps.snippet: # [doc = " How trustworthy a value's representation/bound is. Honesty is monotone-downward: an operation's"] # [doc = " result is never stronger than its weakest input (the [`meet`](GuaranteeStrength::meet))."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `serde` form is the bare string `\"Exact\"|\"Proven\"|\"Empirical\"|\"Declared\"`"] # [doc = " (`guarantee.schema.json`)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq , serde :: Serialize , serde :: Deserialize)] pub enum GuaranteeStrength { # [doc = " No approximation; `bound == None` (M-I1)."] Exact , # [doc = " Approximate with a machine-checked bound (basis `ProvenThm`)."] Proven , # [doc = " Approximate with an empirically-validated bound (basis `EmpiricalFit`)."] Empirical , # [doc = " Approximate with a user-asserted, unvalidated bound (basis `UserDeclared`); always flagged."] Declared , }
gaps.reason: enum `GuaranteeStrength` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 — every value already copies structurally; DN-128 §6.1) — not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: impl GuaranteeStrength { # [doc = " The strongest strength — the identity of [`meet`](Self::meet) and the unit of"] # [doc = " [`meet_all`](Self::meet_all): `meet(Exact, g) == g` for all `g`."] pub const TOP : GuaranteeStrength = GuaranteeStrength :: Exact ; # [doc = " All four strengths, strongest-to-weakest — for exhaustive iteration in tests and tooling."] pub const ALL : [GuaranteeStrength ; 4] = [GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical , GuaranteeStrength :: Declared ,] ; # [doc = " Lattice rank, `0` = strongest (`Exact`) … `3` = weakest (`Declared`). The [`meet`](Self::meet)"] # [doc = " of two strengths is the one with the larger rank (the weakest)."] # [must_use] pub fn rank (self) -> u8 { match self { GuaranteeStrength :: Exact => 0 , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven => 1 , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical => 2 , GuaranteeStrength :: Declared => 3 , } } # [doc = " The lattice **meet** (greatest lower bound): the *weakest* of `self` and `other`"] # [doc = " (RFC-0001 §3.4/§4.7). Honesty is monotone-downward — a result can never be stronger than"] # [doc = " its weakest input — so composition takes the meet. The meet is the strength with the"] # [doc = " larger [`rank`](Self::rank); on a tie the operands are equal, so either is returned."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Algebraically a meet-semilattice: **commutative**, **associative**, **idempotent**, with"] # [doc = " identity [`TOP`](Self::TOP) (`Exact`). These laws are verified by *exhaustion* over all"] # [doc = " 4×4(×4) tuples — the value space is finite, so this is a complete check, not sampling."] # [must_use] pub fn meet (self , other : GuaranteeStrength) -> GuaranteeStrength { if self . rank () >= other . rank () { self } else { other } } # [doc = " Propagate guarantees through an operation (RFC-0001 §4.7):"] # [doc = " `guarantee(result) = meet(guarantee(v_1), …, guarantee(v_n), g_f)`, where `g_f` is the"] # [doc = " operation's own intrinsic strength. Folds [`meet`](Self::meet) over the inputs starting"] # [doc = " from `intrinsic`. With no inputs the result is `intrinsic` itself."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the *only* sanctioned way to derive an operation's result strength — disclosure can"] # [doc = " only degrade, never spuriously upgrade (VR-3/VR-5)."] # [must_use] pub fn propagate (intrinsic : GuaranteeStrength , inputs : impl IntoIterator < Item = GuaranteeStrength > ,) -> GuaranteeStrength { inputs . into_iter () . fold (intrinsic , GuaranteeStrength :: meet) } # [doc = " The meet of a sequence of strengths, weakest-wins, starting from [`TOP`](Self::TOP)"] # [doc = " (`Exact`). An empty sequence yields `Exact` (the identity). Equivalent to"] # [doc = " [`propagate`](Self::propagate) with an `Exact` intrinsic."] # [must_use] pub fn meet_all (strengths : impl IntoIterator < Item = GuaranteeStrength >) -> GuaranteeStrength { Self :: propagate (GuaranteeStrength :: TOP , strengths) } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: GuaranteeStrength ; use super :: GuaranteeStrength :: { Declared , Empirical , Exact , Proven } ; # [test] fn ranks_are_strongest_to_weakest () { assert ! (Exact . rank () < Proven . rank ()) ; assert ! (Proven . rank () < Empirical . rank ()) ; assert ! (Empirical . rank () < Declared . rank ()) ; } # [doc = " The meet equals the weakest (larger-rank) operand — for every one of the 16 pairs."] # [test] fn meet_is_weakest_for_all_pairs () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { for b in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { let m = a . meet (b) ; assert_eq ! (m . rank () , a . rank () . max (b . rank ()) , "meet({a:?},{b:?})") ; assert ! (m == a || m == b) ; } } } # [doc = " **Commutativity**, exhaustively over all 4×4 pairs."] # [test] fn meet_is_commutative () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { for b in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (a . meet (b) , b . meet (a) , "commutativity {a:?},{b:?}") ; } } } # [doc = " **Idempotence**, all 4."] # [test] fn meet_is_idempotent () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (a . meet (a) , a , "idempotence {a:?}") ; } } # [doc = " **Associativity**, exhaustively over all 4×4×4 triples."] # [test] fn meet_is_associative () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { for b in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { for c in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (a . meet (b) . meet (c) , a . meet (b . meet (c)) , "associativity {a:?},{b:?},{c:?}") ; } } } } # [doc = " **Identity**: `Exact` (`TOP`) is the unit on both sides, for every strength."] # [test] fn exact_is_the_identity () { assert_eq ! (GuaranteeStrength :: TOP , Exact) ; for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (Exact . meet (a) , a , "left identity {a:?}") ; assert_eq ! (a . meet (Exact) , a , "right identity {a:?}") ; } } # [doc = " `Declared` is the bottom: it absorbs everything (the weakest is always weakest)."] # [test] fn declared_is_absorbing () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (Declared . meet (a) , Declared , "{a:?}") ; assert_eq ! (a . meet (Declared) , Declared , "{a:?}") ; } } # [doc = " `propagate` realizes RFC-0001 §4.7: the result is the meet of all inputs and the intrinsic"] # [doc = " strength — an `Empirical` input drags an otherwise-`Exact` op down to `Empirical`."] # [test] fn propagate_takes_the_meet_of_inputs_and_intrinsic () { assert_eq ! (GuaranteeStrength :: propagate (Exact , [Exact , Empirical , Exact]) , Empirical) ; assert_eq ! (GuaranteeStrength :: propagate (Proven , [Exact , Exact]) , Proven) ; assert_eq ! (GuaranteeStrength :: propagate (Declared , []) , Declared) ; assert_eq ! (GuaranteeStrength :: propagate (Proven , [Exact , Declared , Empirical]) , Declared) ; } # [doc = " `meet_all` is `propagate` from the `Exact` identity; empty → `Exact`."] # [test] fn meet_all_folds_from_exact () { assert_eq ! (GuaranteeStrength :: meet_all ([]) , Exact) ; assert_eq ! (GuaranteeStrength :: meet_all ([Proven , Empirical]) , Empirical) ; assert_eq ! (GuaranteeStrength :: meet_all ([Exact , Proven , Exact]) , Proven) ; } }
